title: Bridge Essay: Hemispheric asymmetries in visual mental imagery.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Hemispheric, asymmetries, in, visual, mental.

**Hemispheric asymmetries in visual mental imagery.** — Brain Struct Funct. PMID: 33885966. DOI: 10.1007/s00429-021-02277-w. Visual mental imagery is the faculty whereby we can "visualize" objects that are not in our line of sight. Longstanding evidence dating back over thirty years has shown that unilateral brain lesions, especially in the left temporal lobe, can impair aspects of this ability. Yet, there is currently no attempt to identify analogies between these neuropsychological findings of hemispheric asymmetry and those from other neuroscientific approaches.
